title: The effect of government interventions on surplus
description: Practice what you have learned about the impact of prrice controls and quotas on consumer surplus, producer surplus, total surplus, and deadweight loss in this exercise.
topic_path: The effects of government interventions in markets
The effect of government interventions on surplus

assessment_items.question_content:
The market for taxi rides has a downward sloping demand and an upward sloping supply.

**What happens in the market for taxi rides if a quota is imposed on this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Producer surplus increases but total surplus decreases.
The marginal benefit of the quantity sold is less than the marginal cost of the quantity sold.
Consumer surplus increases and total surplus increases.
Consumer surplus increases but total surplus decreases.

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph shows the market for cheese sandwiches. 
![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22336592f1ec8710861d1f6111a17f9dbf9f3796)

Assume the government has imposed a quota of $Q_1$ on the market for sandwiches.

**What areas on the graph represent producer surplus and consumer surplus?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Producer surplus is $B+C+D$; consumer surplus is $A$.
Producer surplus is $C+D+F$; consumer surplus is $A+B+E$.
Producer surplus is $D$; consumer surplus is $A+B+C$.
Producer surplus is $E$; consumer surplus is $F$.
Producer surplus is $C+D+F$; consumer surplus is $E$.

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph shows the market for cheese sandwiches. 
![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22336592f1ec8710861d1f6111a17f9dbf9f3796)

**If the government has imposed a quota of $Q_1$ on the market for sandwiches, what do the areas E and F represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: E represents deadweight loss from consumers no longer able to participate in the market; F represents deadweight loss from producers no longer able to participate in the market.
E represents producer surplus gained at the expense of consumers; F represents producer surplus that is unaffected by the quota.
E represents consumer surplus gained at the expense of producers; F represents consumer surplus that is unaffected by the quota.
E represents tax revenue gained at the expense of consumers; F represents the tax revenue gained at the expense of producers.
E represents the efficiency loss to producers; F represents the efficiency loss to consumers.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/15daf7a8675e068c4b6dbf73b77065d46eeed59d)

**What area(s) depict the deadweight loss that results from producers who are no longer able to participate in this market?**



[[☃ radio 1]]

This graph shows the market for fried bologna sandwiches.
 
![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/15daf7a8675e068c4b6dbf73b77065d46eeed59d)

**Which area(s) are reallocated *from* consumers *to* producers if a binding price floor is imposed on this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Why will a binding price ceiling result in an inefficient allocation of resources?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: At the quantity exchanged $MB>MC$ and there is a shortage in the market.
At the quantity exchanged, $MB>MC$ and there is a surplus.
At the quantity exchanged, $MB<MC$ and there is a shortage.
At the quantity exchanged, $MC=MB$ and there is a shortage.
At the quantity exchanged, $MB<MC$ and there is a surplus.

options.alt: A line graph illustrates the equilibrium in a market, showing the relationship between price, P, on the Y-axis and quantity, Q, on the X-axis. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 28 with increments of 2, and the horizontal axis ranges from 0 to 16 with increments of 2. Two lines are displayed, the supply line, labeled S equals M C, slopes upward from left to right, and the demand line, labeled D equals M B, slopes downward from left to right. assessment_items.question_content:
This graph shows the market for pepper jelly scones. A price ceiling of $\$16$ has been imposed in this market.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6788d80b7460ce3fe513347bc7f849234baeac97)

**What is the value of consumer surplus after the price ceiling is imposed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Consumer surplus is the area beneath the demand curve and above the price paid ($\$16$), as indicated by the shaded area in this graph:

![A line graph illustrates the equilibrium in a market, showing the relationship between price, P, on the Y-axis and quantity, Q, on the X-axis. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 40 with increments of 4, and the horizontal axis ranges from 0 to 12 with increments of 3. Two lines are displayed, the supply line, labeled S, slopes upward from left to right, and the demand line, labeled D, slopes downward from left to right. Key intersections occur at the following points, (6, 16), (9, 22), and (12, 28). Dashed lines extend from these intersections horizontally to the Y-axis and vertically to the X-axis, forming a grid. A shaded area is enclosed below the D line and above the horizontal dashed lines extending from (12, 28) and (9, 22).zontal dashed lines extending from (12, 28) and (9, 22).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3d5ddfe3ab3f18961d4c2735557fe4f8f29348df)
We can divide the shaded area into a rectangle and a triangle to more easily calculate consumer surplus:

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/83362f120189c2b29eb5b7a62a2f9d5d1548f9a7)
The area of a triangle is:

$\text{Area of a triangle} = \dfrac{1}{2}\times \text{base}\times \text{height}$

The area of a rectangle is:

$\text{Area of a rectangle} = \text{base} \times \text{height}$
The base of the triangle and the rectangle is the quantity sold: $6$.

The height of the triangle is \$12:

$\$40-\$28=\$12$.

The height of the rectangle is \$12:

$\$28-\$16=\$12$
Consumer surplus is the sum of the area of the triangle and the area of the rectangle:

$\begin{align} \text{Area of the triangle} &=\dfrac{1}{2} \times 6 \times \$12\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2} \$72\\\\
&=\$36\\\\
\text{Area of the rectangle} &=6\times\$12\\\\
&=\$72\\\\
\text{Consumer surplus}&=\$36 +\$72\\\\
&=\$108\end{align}$
Consumer surplus is $\$108$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A price floor of $\$44$ has been imposed on this market:

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c4002589c88afa327ed8b1d1aa76465dbbaa7678)

**What is the value of the deadweight loss that occurs as a result of sellers who are no longer able to participate in this market?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Deadweight loss from sellers no longer participating in the market is the area that used to be part of producer surplus that is now deadweight loss, as illustrated in the shaded area in this graph:

![A line graph illustrates the equilibrium in a market, showing the relationship between price, P, on the Y-axis and quantity, Q, on the X-axis. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 60 with increments of 5, and the horizontal axis ranges from 0 to 11 with increments of 1. Two lines are displayed, the supply line, labeled S, slopes upward from left to right, and the demand line, labeled D, slopes downward from left to right. Key intersections occur at the following points, (8, 29) and (11, 38). Dashed lines extend from these intersections horizontally to the Y-axis and vertically to the X-axis, forming a grid. A shaded triangular area is enclosed between the S and D lines, indicating producer surplus or price-related effects.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9ada1c6a5a3bf2671eaa88e40928cda42e2d2f29)
The area of a triangle is:

$\text{Area of a triangle} = \dfrac{1}{2} \times (\text{base} \times \text{height})$

The base of this triangle is the quantity that is no longer sold:

$11-8=3$

The height of this triangle is $\$9$:

$\$38 - \$29 = \$9$
The area of this triangle is $\$13.50$:

$\begin{align} \text{Area of triangle } &=\dfrac{1}{2} \times (\text{base} \times \text{height})\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \times (3 \times \$9)\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2} \times \$27\\\\
&=\$13.50\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The market shown here has a quota of $4$ units.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the value of deadweight loss in this market as a result of the quota?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The deadweight loss in this graph is area $U$, which is a triangle.
The area of a triangle is:

$\text{Area of a triangle} = \dfrac{1}{2} \times (\text{base} \times \text{height})$
The base of the purple triangle is the difference between the equilibrium quantity and the quantity sold with the quota:

$8-4=4$

The height of the triangle is $\$6$:

$\$9 - \$3 = \$6$

The deadweight loss that occurs due to the quota of 4 units is 
$\begin{align} \text{Deadweight loss} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \times 4 \times \$6\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2} \times \$24\\\\
&=\$12\end{align}$

options.alt: A line graph illustrates the equilibrium in a market, showing the relationship between price, P, on the Y-axis and quantity, Q, on the X-axis. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 14 with increments of 1, and the horizontal axis ranges from 0 to 14 with increments of 1. Two lines are displayed, the upward-sloping supply line labeled S and the downward-sloping demand line labeled D. Key intersections occur at the following points, (3, 9), (7, 5), and (10, 3). Dashed lines extend from these intersections horizontally to the Y-axis and vertically to the X-axis, forming a grid. Shaded areas are labeled S, T, U, and V.
